name: Electroplating Bath — Faraday Deposition Rate, Plating Time & Rectifier Power
subtitle: Surface Engineering | Manufacturing — ISO 2081:2018 (zinc electroplating), ASTM B633-19 (zinc on iron/steel)

inScope: Electroplating Bath — Faraday Deposition Rate, Plating Time & Rectifier Power
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Total Part Surface Area to be Plated (A_part)

standards.name: Electroplating Bath — Faraday Deposition Rate, Plating Time & Rectifier Power
standards.description: Electroplating Bath — Faraday Deposition Rate, Plating Time & Rectifier Power — Surface Engineering, Manufacturing

formulas.expression: const METALS = {Zn:{rho:7.13,M:65.38,n:2}, Ni:{rho:8.91,M:58.69,n:2}, Cr:{rho:7.19,M:52.0,n:6}, Cu:{rho:8.96,M:63.55,n:2}, Au:{rho:19.32,M:197.0,n:1}, Ag:{rho:10.49,M:107.87,n:1}}; const m = METALS[metal_deposited]; E_eq_g_Ah = m.M / (m.n * 26.8)   // [g/A·h] Metal Electrochemical Equivalent (E_eq) — Faraday's Law Reference: Faraday's law: E_eq = M/(n·F/3600) = M/(n·26.8); F=96485 C/mol — ISO 2081 §3.7

const m2 = METALS[metal_deposited]; r_dep_um_min = E_eq_g_Ah * current_density_a_dm2 * cathode_efficiency_pct / 100 * 10000 / (m2.rho * 60)   // [µm/min] Deposition Rate at Given Current Density (r_dep) Reference: r = E_eq·i·η_c / (ρ·60) [µm/min]; i in A/dm², ρ in g/cm³ — Faraday deposition rate

formulas.reference: I = i × A_cathode — total current for all parts in bath simultaneously

formulas.expression: const m3 = METALS[metal_deposited]; W_deposit_g = E_eq_g_Ah * I_rect_a * t_plate_min / 60 * cathode_efficiency_pct / 100; W_anode_g = W_deposit_g / (cathode_efficiency_pct / 100) * 1.05   // [g] Metal Mass Deposited per Batch & Anode Consumption Reference: W = E_eq·I·t·η_c; anode consumption ≈ W_deposit / η_c × 1.05 (5% drag-out and oxidation loss)

warnings.message: Chromium cathode efficiency > 30%: unusually high for hexavalent Cr bath — verify input; trivalent Cr (Cr³⁺) baths achieve 80–95% efficiency but require different parameters
Rectifier current > 10,000 A: industrial-scale rectifier — specify thyristor or IGBT rectifier with ±1% voltage regulation; busbar cross-section critical for voltage uniformity
High power input at elevated bath temperature: Joule heating will raise bath temperature — specify bath cooling system capacity as Q_cool ≈ P_rect × 0.6 kW
Gold plating > 5 µm: very high material cost — verify specification requires full gold; consider gold flash (0.5 µm) over nickel undercoat for connector applications per MIL-G-45204
Low CD on large area: very long plating time — evaluate rack density, multiple simultaneous racks, or continuous plating line for high-volume parts

verdictText.warn: PASS — High Utilization: Peer Review Required
